hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/ECL3/TM5/TM6 regions, while mutating position 6 to acidic residues (E/D) will disrupt off-target (SSTR3/SSTR5) electrostatic interactions, thereby increasing the selectivity margin.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is that strong ddG alone is insufficient for success; selectivity must be explicitly optimized by disrupting off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions. The next iteration will focus on these positions to increase Δmargin.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are likely involved in off-target interactions with SSTR3 and SSTR5. Focusing mutations here can disrupt conserved pocket contacts while preserving key pharmacophore residues.
Introducing charged or hydrophobic residues at these positions can disrupt conserved interactions with off-target SSTRs while preserving the pharmacophore core (FWKT at 7-10) and Cys3/Cys14.
